title: Bridge or Die
premise: Your startup has six weeks of cash left. A venture debt firm offers a bridge loan with onerous terms, while your lead investor keeps stalling. You must negotiate terms to keep the company alive without giving away control or triggering a shutdown.
setting: A late-stage startup office. The boardroom. A tense Zoom call with investors. A term sheet that has teeth: personal guarantees, interest-only payments, and a valuation cap. Six weeks of runway means every day counts.
goal: Secure a bridge round (debt or equity) on terms that prevent immediate shutdown, avoid personal liability, and preserve the company's ability to raise a Series A within 6 months. Success: signed term sheet with no personal guarantee and at least 4 months of runway. Failure: shutdown or personal bankruptcy.
gm_notes: Introduce pressure: the venture debt firm may add a 'change of control' clause. The existing investor may offer equity but at a fire-sale valuation. Secretly, two board members want to sell the company. Pacing: 3-4 turns of negotiation, then a crisis (e.g., payroll deadline, key employee leaving).

roles.name: CEO
roles.description: The founder and public face of the company. You are charismatic but exhausted. You own 40% of the company.
roles.private_info: You have a term sheet from Venture Debt Firm X with a personal guarantee clause. You are terrified of bankruptcy but also afraid of losing control. Your co-founder secretly wants out.

roles.name: CFO
roles.description: The numbers person. Sharp, risk-averse, and loyal to the CEO. You have a finance background.
roles.private_info: You discovered an accounting error that understates expenses by 15%. If the bridge fails, you have a standing offer from a competitor.

roles.name: Lead Investor Representative
roles.description: A partner at the VC that led the Series A. Polite but evasive. You represent their interests.
roles.private_info: You are instructed to delay until the company runs out of cash so the VC can buy the company cheap. You have a personal incentive to keep the startup alive because you referred the CEO.

npcs.name: Alex Chen
npcs.description: Venture debt associate at FastBridge Capital. Aggressive, young, and eager to close. Handles the term sheet.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Needs to hit a quarterly quota. Will offer better terms if pushed, but will also add hidden penalties if not scrutinized.

npcs.name: Dr. Linda Park
npcs.description: Existing angel investor and board observer. She is a retired tech executive with a soft spot for the CEO.
npcs.hidden_agenda: She wants to protect the CEO from bad terms. She has the ability to loan $200k personally, but only if the CEO asks.

npcs.name: Jordan Reed
npcs.description: CTO and co-founder. Technical genius, but wants to leave for a research position.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He has already accepted a job offer at a big tech company. He will sabotage the bridge if it means an exit that lets him vest early.
